behavior_summary: WARDEW::look: When the wardrobe is examined (U8P_WARDEW::look event) the script composes the literal string "wardrobe " and asks the item to 'bark' that string to the viewer/player so the player sees the object's short description.

WARDEW::use: When the wardrobe is used/activated the script reads the item's status word, tests bit 0x0100 and uses that to decide whether to open or close the wardrobe UI/gump: bit set -> open gump with parameter 0x0D, bit clear -> close gump.
Behavior is localized to item operations (Item::bark, Item::getStatus, Item::openGump, Item::closeGump) and does not modify global flags in this excerpt.
notable_control_flow: Event-driven: WARDEW::look runs on the 'U8P_WARDEW::look' (examine) event and returns after sending the bark; a small returned-value check and control-flow/suspend/pop sequence occurs after the bark call.
WARDEW::use performs a synchronous read of Item::getStatus, masks bit 0x0100, and conditionally calls Item::openGump(0x0D) or Item::closeGump based on that bit.
No inter-function control transfers between the two handlers in this excerpt; each event handler operates independently on the item instance parameter.
open_questions: Which PID (viewer) is passed into WARDEW::look at runtime — the disassembly shows a pid pushed but the exact source/process is not explicit.
What exact semantic meaning does status bit 0x0100 represent for this item (inferred to indicate a gump/open state but not formally documented)?
What is the user-visible content/structure of the gump opened with parameter 0x0D (0x0D meaning inferred but not described here)?
Are there any side-effects (animation, sounds, or linked global state changes) when the gump opens/closes that are handled elsewhere?

main_function.name: U8P_WARDEW::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the wardrobe's short description to a viewer by composing the literal "wardrobe " and invoking the item's bark routine so the viewer/player sees that text.
entry_conditions: Event 0 / U8P_WARDEW::look: triggered when an actor/avatar examines or 'looks' at the wardrobe object (fast-area/U8P_WARDEW::look event).

function_map.name: U8P_WARDEW::look
function_map.role: Event handler for 'U8P_WARDEW::look' that composes the text "wardrobe " and calls Item::bark to display it to the inspecting player/process.

function_map.name: U8P_WARDEW::use
function_map.role: Event handler for 'use' that reads the item's status word and toggles the wardrobe gump: open (Item::openGump(0x0D)) when bit 0x0100 is set, otherwise close (Item::closeGump()).

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine/item routine that displays or sends a short descriptive message (bark) to a player/process; used by U8P_WARDEW::look handlers to show item descriptions.

function_map.name: Item::getStatus
function_map.role: Returns the item's status flags/word; used to inspect bit 0x0100 in the use handler.

function_map.name: Item::openGump
function_map.role: Opens the item's UI/gump with a provided parameter; called with 0x0D when status bit 0x0100 is set.

function_map.name: Item::closeGump
function_map.role: Closes the item's UI/gump; called when status bit 0x0100 is clear.

external_calls.purpose: Send/display the wardrobe's short description string ("wardrobe ") to the viewer/player.
Retrieve the item's status flags to test bit 0x0100 which determines gump state.
Open the wardrobe's UI/gump with parameter 0x0D when the status bit 0x0100 is set.
Close the wardrobe's UI/gump when the status bit 0x0100 is clear.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: WARDEW::look: When the wardrobe is examined (U8P_WARDEW::look event) the script composes the literal string "wardrobe " and asks the item to 'bark' that string to the viewer/player so the player sees the object's short description.
